title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authorized inclusive site-audit charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.
Record who controls each target, permitted identities, paths, methods, times, data uses, and revocation contact before any automated request.
Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Stakeholder Decision
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 2671 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.

Inspect the Stakeholder Decision trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Stakeholder Decision.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Stakeholder Decision, keep Stakeholder Decision connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
